reviews.review_text: What a well maintained caravan park in beautiful Cape Glouster. We were welcomed by Kathy and she was a gem of information on the weather, fishing and restaurants in the area. We were unpowered, and the sites were grassy with shady & sunny spots because they are long and can be driven through. All amenities, camp kitchen, laundry were spotless, the pool was sparkly and refreshingly cool. You can walk to the bowls club, 200m away, and if you dine at Montes Beach Bar (highly recommend) Montes have a courtesy bus. 100% enjoyed our stay and will definitely return. Thanks!

Fantastic spot! Would recommend trying to stay at the back end of the park to avoid the road noise but otherwise 10/10.

The pool was beautiful and clean, kitchen was spotless and the location is wonderful. Highly recommend getting the courtesy bus over to the resort for a sunset drink.

As others have mentioned, plenty of rules but it does keep the park in excellent condition.

Dropped by to the convience store to purchase tampons and a water.

Asked where the bathrooms were. Was refused entry as its 'private property'. Then was directed to the public bathrooms nearby at the bowls club. I reiterated I needed to use the bathroom to use the purchased tampons. The man serving me then relentlessly finally directed me to the 'private property' toilets.

Felt terrible for being a woman.

What a beautiful peaceful location. The park was very well maintained and clean.
Neil and Kathy were more than helpful and a pleasure to chat to.
I spent 3 nights in my caravan with my dog, and spent the days walking down to the beach and fishing.. the beaches are pristine and the water is so clear.
I really enjoyed my stay and will definitely be going back again.

We had a fantastic stay at Hydeaway Bay Caravan & Camping Park. It’s a great little park in a beautiful location and very well set up for travellers.

The campsites are large with a mix of well-maintained grass areas and concrete slabs, all with water and power hookups. The grass is kept in excellent condition and the whole park is tidy and well looked after. Amenities are clean, and it’s great having a washing machine and clothes dryer available for longer stays.

There’s a really good outdoor kitchen with two electric BBQs and even a pool, making it a nice social area to relax after a day out. Fishermen will also appreciate the fish cleaning table. We also had strong 4G Telstra service the whole time, which is a bonus for staying connected.

The park is dog friendly (dogs on lead at all times), which makes it perfect if you’re travelling with pets.

It’s only a short walk to beautiful Hideaway Bay Beach, and you can also walk to Hideaway Bay Bowls Club for afternoon drinks on Friday and Saturday from 3:30 pm.

From here you can easily take day trips using the courtesy buses to some great local spots like Dingo Beach Hotel, The Beach Bar and Restaurant at Montes, or Cape Gloucester Resort. All are beautiful beachside venues with great food and cocktails, and the Friday night raffle at the Dingo Pub is always a bit of fun.

Overall, a fantastic caravan and camping park in a great location. Perfect for relaxing, fishing, beach walks, and enjoying the laid-back Whitsunday lifestyle. Highly recommended!
Great place to stay! Close to the beautiful Cape Gloucester

We stayed for a week over xmas/new year and had a lovely week! Immaculately kept site with very clean amenities, pet friendly and also a pool! Walking distance to beautiful beach and Restaurants to eat at Montes, Cape Gloucester and Dingo beach but otherwise bring plenty of supplies as nearest shops approx 30min away!

This is an absolute gem of a caravan park, situated in the most beautiful part of the Whitsunday area. The gardens and sites are immaculately kept, there is a large camp kitchen and clean modern amenities. There are no chemicals allowed as the dump point empties into a septic tank, so either do not put chemicals in your cassette whilst I. The caravan park or use those suitable for septic tanks. The sites, some of which are grass and some of which are grass with concrete slab, and spacious and easy to access. The gorgeous beaches of the Hideaway Bay and Dingo Beach areas are all within walking distance or a short drive in the car. This park has been under new ownership since 2022 and the new owners have built the communal areas and added a lovely pool - not huge but big enough to do a few lengths, and also cool off in the hotter times! The hosts have been charming and helpful, and I really recommend this park to anyone who prefers a smaller quieter caravan park in lovely countryside to the Big 4’s and Discovery Parks type of parks! Please ignore the dreadful review shown on Tripadvisor which was written several years ago and is completely out of date.
Very friendly owners, nothing too much trouble, and a lovely cool pool, very very clean amenities, enjoyed our week there.  Highly recommend
Stayed in early September great place location10/10.

We had a great stay in the caravan for a couple of nights at Hydeaway Bay,
What a fantastic location and park, the kids and myself loved the pool, amenities were nice and clean - put the boat in for a fish at dingo beach 🏝️
And could wash the motor out back at the park. 👌
Well done! Will be back some time for sure
Really enjoyed our couple of nights here. Lovely grounds and a very quiet and relaxing atmosphere. Will be back. Thanks

Great location to visit surrounding beaches however it is not family friendly at all. Think they would prefer no kids there.
A lot of strict rules which makes you a little on edge and hard to relax

CAMP CRANKY
As a local I truly hope everyone visiting this part of the world knows the rest of ‘Whitsundays’ aren’t like the couple who run this camp.

My parents came to visit with their little dog and decided to stay at the Hydeaway caravan park. We had stay before with previous owners/care takers - they were absolutely beautiful people.
So naturally we didn’t think we’d need to read the reviews, we wished we had.

Without going into too much detail, make sure you get a list of all the ‘allowed to do’s’ (it’ll be shorter than the don’ts list) and adhere to them. Otherwise you will get an unpleasant visit from the park sheriff’s, Kathy and Neil.

In all seriousness;
I’m all for understanding places have rules - that is so fine. You dont however need to treat people like they’re criminals within your park. There are other ways to approach situations and to talk to people.
Advice to owners - take a crash course on customer service, it might do you some good. It also doesn’t hurt or smile or be nice. Don’t forget you’re running a holiday park and representing the Whitsundays.

Future visitors - If you find yourself visiting this part of Australia, find a different park. One that accepts fun and has better hospitality. There are plenty of other parks, airbnb’s and other accommodation alternatives that will make you feel like you’re on a relaxing holiday, instead of tip toeing around and trying not to get into trouble.
Everyone else in the Whitsundays would love to have you here!

Shockingly Rude and Aggressive Management - Avoid This Place.

We recently visited to pick up a friend staying at the park and were stunned by the behaviour of the owner. Despite having a clearly displayed disabled sticker and helping someone with serious mobility issues (two recent knee reconstructions), we were aggressively confronted as we were leaving. The owner ran out, visibly angry, and launched into a verbal tirade before even asking what was happening.

There was no opportunity to explain the disability or the reason for stopping near the caravan, and no interest shown in understanding — only hostility. The encounter left us shaken. We understand that parks have rules about parking and speed limits, but there’s a way to enforce them respectfully. This wasn’t it.

Our friends staying at the park had already mentioned the same pattern: micromanagement, unfriendly treatment, and a generally hostile attitude towards guests. Sadly, we experienced it firsthand.

If you’re after a genuinely friendly, relaxed, and welcoming atmosphere, head to Tropical Caravan Park in Bowen instead — it’s run by lovely people who actually understand hospitality. The owners here could learn a lot about what it means to make guests feel welcome rather than running the place like an internment camp.

As someone who spent years working in the region’s hospitality industry, promoting the Whitsunday’s this is the first time I’ve ever left a review saying go elsewhere.

Worst caravan park I have ever stayed at hands down. READ THE REVIEWS. Absolutely shocking. There are rules and signs everywhere you look. God forbid you go 1km over their speed limit, park in the wrong spot, make a noise. You have a phone call coming straight in. They watch you like hawks. The owners need to chill out and not be so uptight. We weren’t aloud to use to showers in the morning because of old love. I would rather sleep in the car.

Absolutely ridiculous. I was at this cavern park for less than four hours and spent the entire time terrified of making any noise. The staff are not only obsessed with silence but also unbelievably rude—acting like they’re doing you a favor by letting you be there. I couldn’t enjoy a single moment without being scolded or treated like a criminal for daring to speak or laugh. What should have been a fun visit turned into a stressful, joyless nightmare. Avoid this place unless you enjoy being micromanaged and insulted, never again!!

Read the reviews and heed the warnings people, the old witch was true to form. These people do not like kids so travelling families beware.

The list of rules and no splashing in the pool T&C upon booking would have been quirky and even comical, but getting drunk and making our kids cry for spotlighting canetoads was next level. Also calling me at 8:30pm demanding money was completely unnecessary.

We are at the end of our lap and this was the worse vibe of all the parks we stayed in Australia, which is a massive call. The weirdest part is their back porch faces the park so she sits there and watches your every move like the eye of sauron.

The Cape Gloster resort was the one saving grace and we cannot wait until this place opens for vans. Great spot and great people and your kids can splash away to their little hearts content.

Friendly welcome from all staff, very clean ver quiet and relaxing
Will stay again
Really nice place to stay. A lovely caravan park with amenities, camp kitchen, swimming pool and no wifi, wich is good for a better immersive experience.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We had an incredible stay at Hideaway Bay Caravan and Camping! From the moment we arrived, Neil and Kathy went above and beyond to make us feel welcome. Their warm hospitality sets the tone for the whole park.

The grounds are immaculate and beautifully maintained, creating a relaxing and friendly atmosphere. The facilities are top-notch — the camp kitchen is one of the best we’ve seen, and the pool is absolutely magnificent after a warm day.

What really surprised us was the amazing bird life all around the park — it adds something special to waking up each morning. The combination of nature, comfort, and genuine hosts makes this place stand out.

Highly recommend staying here — we’ll definitely be back!

We recently had the misfortune of staying at this campground, and I must say, it was an experience I'll never forget. Upon arrival, we were handed a rule book longer than a court transcript. The owners seem to have a passion for enforcing these rules in a manner that is considered rude and arrogant and it's clear they're more interested in control than creating a welcoming atmosphere.
The constant monitoring and lack of privacy were overwhelming. On multiple occasions, we witnessed the male owner watching the cameras in the pool area where people were swimming, which feels like a serious invasion of privacy! The owners' behavior is polarizing, with the wife being particularly enthusiastic about enforcing the rules and yelling at people as they enter the park in an abusive manner - this kind of behavior is completely unacceptable and unwelcoming. Multiple guests echoed the same sentiment: the park owners' micromanaging style and rudeness were off-putting. No surprise they'll never return either! When the park isn't booked out during school holidays, it speaks volumes. Especially given the location! One thing that struck us as odd was the owners' habit of drinking in the communal fire pit area and discussing negative reviews with customers? We were shocked at how forthcoming she was about it and made us feel very awkward. It seemed like they were using the area for their own leisure rather than creating a welcoming space for guests.  The toilets didnt have hand towels and/or dryers, which was a significant inconvenience. The stench coming from the tanks was unbearable at times. The lack of activities or events was also a letdown. We stayed for a long weekend, and there was nothing organized to bring campers together. No communal gatherings, no games, no events – just a bunch of people sitting in their sites, feeling unwelcome and disconnected from the constant micromanagement of the female manager. The reviews online paint a consistent picture of a campground with deep-seated issues. Complaints about the rules, the lack of flexibility, and the owner's defensive responses are all consistent with our experience. What's even more telling is that the owner responds to every negative review, but instead of addressing the concerns, she shifts the blame to the guests. It's clear that she's not interested in listening to feedback or improving the experience for her customers. The campground itself is in a stunning location, and the facilities are good. However, the owners' attitude and management style make it a place we'd never recommend and we are local to the area. Not to mention that the locals seem to share our sentiments, and it's clear this campground has a reputation in the area already! If you're looking for a relaxing camping experience, keep looking. We're excited to try the new campground in town and won't be returning here anytime soon. It's a shame, really, because the area is beautiful, and the campground has potential. But until the management changes, we'd advise others to steer clear, especially if you have children! Although we never had a direct run in with the female manager, its very clear to see the issues within the park and why other paying customers had theirs.

Response in photos to Manager's comments!
Great place to stay, we stayed 4 nights with van

Our second visit here. Yes there's lots of rules imposed, but they are for the safety and comfort of patrons. This is THE only heated pool we've struck in nearly 4 months of travel and was very welcomed. Yes pools get very cold in the northern winter! Good camp kitchen and a great communal fire pit. Bathrooms daggy but clean.
Nice place and beaches

Loved our 3rd stay here. The park is immaculately maintained, quiet and spacious. The owners are lovely and make sure the park remains relaxing, safe and clean.
The amenities are clean and showers are hot. There is a pool, shop and great book swap on site. The fire pit is lit every night and everyone is welcome to bring a drink and kick back.
Easy walk to a beautiful beach.
I don’t think this would be a park I would bring young children to, as there is not much for them to do.

Had a great time with my family at Hydeaway Bay. Site was spacious, amenities well looked after and the camp kitchen had everything you needed. Was nice to have a dip in the pool too. Nice and quiet and a great spot to relax. Would love to come back when back in the area again.

It’s a good spot if you’re older but being a young couple that camp around a lot the owners definitely make you feel watched and judged. The old women that drives around on the buggy cleaning up is very rude, watches you when they drive past the camp like you’re being judged by them, stops at the camp for a few seconds everyday and then takes off, feel like we were being monitored the entire time we were there. We hear them saying hi to other campers and waving yet we get ignored. Definitely don’t recommend this campground for a young couple, won’t be back!

I don’t usually write reviews, but I feel the need to warn others before booking here.||The park itself is in a beautiful location with clean amenities - but that’s where the positives end. Earlier this year we stayed as a family of 4 with our dog (well behaved, always on leash, even has his own swag) and had an awful experience with management.||Hoping it was a one off, we gave it another try last night with friends, filling 3 powered sites. Unfortunately, it was worse. Our kids were riding their bikes and, while we acknowledge that maybe some rules weren’t followed by the children, the way the situation was handled was unacceptable. Instead of approaching us as parents in a kind and reasonable way, the owner chose to berate the children directly. Unnecessarily rude and unprofessional.||There are far too many rules enforced in a heavy-handed manner. The message is clear: don’t bring kids or dogs if you want to enjoy your stay.

If you’ve ever dreamed of escaping to paradise only to be barked at like a school child the minute you arrive, then congratulations — Hydeaway Bay Caravan Park is exactly the experience you’re looking for.

Let’s start with the only redeeming quality: the location. It’s genuinely beautiful — secluded beaches, kangaroos bouncing by, birds singing… the sort of place that should leave you relaxed and glowing. Unfortunately, that’s all promptly undone the moment you have to interact with The Manager (you’ll know her when you hear the tone).

From the moment we checked in, it felt less like a holiday and more like checking into a detention center. We were met with a scowl and a barked list of rules, delivered with the warmth of a parking inspector on a power trip. We weren’t greeted, we were warned — about everything from mats on the grass to how quietly we had to breathe after 8 p.m.

Want to sit by the campfire and laugh with friends? Don’t. Want to let your kids enjoy the pool? Good luck. Want to put a toe out of line? She’s watching — and if you’re really lucky, she might film you for her personal collection of “Campers Being Naughty.”

The facilities? Passable. The wildlife? Lovely. The vibe? Ruined by management that treats paying guests like a nuisance. It’s almost impressive how effectively one person can turn a peaceful destination into a place you want to leave early.

Honestly, I’d come back to Hideaway Bay — just not to this park. There are plenty of places in Queensland where the staff still understand hospitality isn’t about control freakery. Until then, this place remains a “Hydeaway” in the worst possible sense.

Tip for management: A smile is free. So is basic respect.
Absolute arrogant greedy owners.|Refused to give a credit for cancellation due to ill health of elderly 88 year old father.|Many similar reviews and would not recommend this as a place to visit.

We recently paid for a single nights caravan accommodation at this park in advance but were unable to attend due to a family illness.
We advised the park as early as possible so they knew and could re-use that site.
We asked if possible could we  revisit the park in a non busy period as we are local to the region and have a credit for this cancellation.
We were bluntly refused and told to refer to the terms and conditions.
Given the extenuating circumstances for cancellation we thought the owners/managers may consider this request.
Obviously GREED far outweighs compassion when these owners/managers make these decisions.
In the long term these GREEDY people will loose out on potential future income from us as well as other Caravan friends we have in this region.

What a fine this place is. Very quiet, hence, there were no kids about and the ones which did have dogs were well behaved. Every thing was clean and the pool area and camp kitchen were excellent. Too all the negative people out there, the owners sets the rules you DO NOT. If you don’t like it, go elsewhere, or you will be politely told to leave. Well done to Kathy and Neil the owners for assistance during our stay. Every night during the winter season there is a communal fire pit where everyone gathers for a chat and some refreshments 🥃. Our first night we were welcome by everyone and it made us feel at home, some have been coming here for 20 years.|Thankyou, we will be back.|Note# they even have proper toilet roll paper, not like that narrow crap at other places. ATD (attention to detail) is very important to us.|Cheers|Grumpy #42A

BEWARE !!!!||Our 4th visit to this park and definitely our last . The staff were fantastic and gladly moved our previously booked stay to this month due to serious medical reasons. We received our revised account on June showing the small amount to pay on our arrival . ||Unfortunately the arrogant, rude and disrespectful manager would not even acknowledge the confirmation account  and winged about staff ever agreeing to it. ||He insisted we pay an extra amount, didn’t even want to look the agreed account and aggressively kicked me out of the office . ||Unfortunately we paid the extra and we will NEVER go to his park again !!! ||With over 50 years of travel around Australia and overseas, we have never ever experienced a person so rude is this||TAKE NOTICE !AND BEWARE
Not too packed in like some caravan parks, nice and quiet and reasonable facilities. Great location, coastline round here is stunning.

Great Caravan Park, the owners take pride in their business. Facilities are clean. Laundry on-site and a small general store. Very good location for people who like fishing. Close proximity to Proserpine and Airlie Beach.

Gorgeous place and very clean.. but full of grey nomad whingers. Cant make a noise after 8pm without being dobbed into the owner.. very unreasonable. Doesn't bother to get the other side of the story  where a person was abused.. instead just threatens to kick people out. Love the place but will never be back

Pros: kiosk, nice camp kitchen, well maintained. Small pool. Cons: riot act read on arrival and at least five "Do not.." signs visible at every point in campsite, doesn't feel welcoming. Incredibly cramped toilets, showers and unusable urinal if you're rugby player sized. Considering remoteness and limited facilities, on and off site, $44 for an unpowered site is ridiculous as that's what you pay for nearby popular tourist sites with far more to do.

This is a small privately owned park.  The owners are meticulous with the grounds and cleanliness.  They have a great camp kitchen area, communal fire pit, swimming pool & a little shop for your last minute needs.  Amenities are very clean.  Bring all your food etc with you or it is  a 20 minute drive back into Proserpine for supplies.  Owners are very friendly and informative about what there is to do around the area.  Current price is $50.00pn for pwd site, which is pretty good for this time of year.||

My husband and I wanted a few days away, and this was sooo relaxing with our old fur baby and bird, we enjoyed the quiet, the gardens and beach. Clean amenities,  friendly staff and owner great. Thanku it was just great. You and Your staff are a great workforce for us visitors to enjoy it.   D. HICKS

The people running the place are the most arrogant, confrontational rude humans i have ever met. ||We will never be back there, its a shame because it is such a nice place in a great location but completely ruined by the behaviour of the staff. Good luck

very very rude managers, playing a family board game was considered to loud for that place.
lady manager walked over filming my family whilst we were playing. do not even bother going here. WASTE OF MONEY!!!
The place is okay, but the manager is absolutely rude. Avoid this place if you can; there are other, much more welcoming places nearby.
Unfriendly , also unfriendly last year with the old owners

We’ve stayed over Easter for three years in a row, but this year will be our last. While I understand there are rules in place over the Easter period, there’s a far better and frankly, more respectful way to speak to paying guests when enforcing them.

Throughout the stay, we felt like we were under constant surveillance, as if we were being scrutinised for possibly doing something wrong. It created an uncomfortable and unwelcoming atmosphere. The wristband policy, in particular, seemed to be enforced selectively. While we personally weren’t approached as we were doing the right thing, members of our family were questioned on the very morning they were leaving after they had already packed up and taken off their bands.

Even more ridiculous was a conversation we had with another guest, who told us the owners had instructed him to lie and claim he was their father to excuse not wearing a wristband. If that’s the level of consistency and honesty being applied, it’s no wonder guests feel singled out and unwelcome.

Rules are one thing we get it. But the attitude and tone with which they’re imposed made it clear that friendliness and hospitality are not a priority here. It’s a shame, because it’s a beautiful location, but we won’t be returning.
Hidden gem! Serenity aplenty. Beautiful beaches a short distance away. Very clean & tidy park.

Fantastic park. Extremely clean, well maintained - gardens are lovely. Quiet, peaceful, a walk to the lovely Hydeaway Bay. Neil was extremely helpful and friendly. Booked in for 2 nights ..stayed for 5 nights. Will definitely be back..soon!
Beautiful location, the park was well maintained and had all the facilities you want. Although, management are rude and the previous negative reviews are 100% correct.
Site is tidy, good location. Not worth putting up with the authoritarian management. Never met such miserable people running a holiday park.
Great place Awesome management by friendly staff  Big thumbs up 👍
Fantastic place lovely staff...Will be back next year. Big thanks

Kathy and Neil have done an amazing job of keeping this van park immaculate.  Stayed 3 nights with our two dachshunds and enjoyed every moment of it. Such a beautiful place to relax and unwind. Will definitely be returning.

Stayed at the park for 2 nights.
Best Caravan park, so clean & well maintained. Caretakers were a great couple, very nice & accomodating, who are taking great pride in their work.
Beautiful grounds & 5 minutes walk to the amazing beach, awesome clean pool & bbq area.
Lovely camping park with clean, working amenities. You can see they really take good care of their property, it looks amazing!

Awful, arrogant and rude mangers wouldn’t recommend to anyone. Lovely place with facilities but  with thier million rules and rudeness and how  disrespectful the managers are I would forget even thinking about going again. Definitely left a sour taste in my mouth.
Lovely place, very helpful whe n checking in went out of their way to make two novice motorhomers feel comfortable.  Good facilities and near dingo Bay.  Loved it thank u happy new year.

Can see why it was the only place not booked out for Christmas, I really wish we did our research and checked all reviews before booking.
As such a nice place this caravan park is to stay at, the owners of the park are not. We adhered to their rules to see all other patrons of the park were able to break them. With a 4 night stay everyday was another warning about something that we had done wrong (in their eyes) They don’t like kids, so don’t travel there with them.. Will never go back there again, nor recommend it.

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Hydeaway Caravan Park, and I must say, it was a fantastic experience! From the moment we arrived, it was clear that guest safety and comfort are top priorities for the management.

I truly appreciated the new wristband policy, which helps ensure that everyone has a safe and enjoyable stay. While some guests seemed to struggle with this rule, I admire how the staff handled the situation with professionalism and grace. It's unfortunate that not everyone understands the importance of these measures, but it’s a testament to the park's commitment to maintaining a safe environment for all.

The park is well-maintained, and the facilities are excellent. It’s evident that the owners put a lot of thought and effort into creating a welcoming atmosphere. I believe that guests who choose to disregard the rules should consider the hard work that goes into running a caravan park, including the costs of maintenance and insurance.

Overall, I highly recommend Hydeaway Caravan Park to anyone looking for a relaxing getaway. Thank you for your dedication and hard work! Keep up the great job, and we look forward to returning!

Fantastic place to stop and stay. Friendly staff who w were able to give us directions for some where for dinner.
Facilities were immaculate.

We loved the location and easy walking access to Hydeaway Bay & Dingo beach from the back of the park. The caravan park was super clean & peaceful. We stayed for 2 night and would come stay here again.

Me and my mate are traveling the east coast and stopped here for a few nights. We had a wonderful time, the facilities were tidy and really nice.

Cathy & Neil are running the camping and are amazing people, it was nice meeting them.

Willem & Mika

Absolutely disgraceful behaviour from the Management at this place.. To live in such a beautiful part of the world & to be so miserable is beyond me. |Some humans are Hidious . |Don’t even bother stopping, keep travelling is all I can say,|EMBARRISING

Awesome place to stay. We've just had over 4 weeks here and have booked for 6 weeks next year. Beaches are beautiful and close, everything is spotlessly clean and tidy, atmosphere in the campground is super friendly and welcoming. Thanks guys for a fantastic stay with you, see you next year. Pete and Rae

We are staying at the HIDEAWAY BAY CARAVAN PARK.  It is BEAUTIFUL! It looks quite new, but I believe it's not.

VERY clean facilities. Friendly, service. Lots of other campers here now.

They cater for vans and campers, with both powered and uncovered sites.

Cabins are NOT AVAILABLE at this location.

PET FRIENDLY, with rules.

We had a fantastic 3 day stay at this beautiful little park. The hosts were Friendly and gave us some great advice of things to do. The park is very clean including the amenities. Beautiful pool area. Do yourself a favour if you are there on a Wednesday and walk across the rd to the bare foot bowls, the locals were friendly, it's a great set up and we had a load of fun. ($5 beers 👍) Cape Gloucester Resort was quite pricey, meals were great though and the location is spot on. Over all we enjoyed our stay at Hydeaway Bay.

Beste lezer,

Zojuist kwamen we hier aan met onze campervan op een later tijdstip dan dat de receptie open was. Vervolgens wilde we kijken of het nog lukte om te bellen, maar de telefoon ging af in de receptie. We hebben vervolgens nog met een ouder stel gesproken die aan het eten waren, en we overlegde dat we dan in de ochtend bij opening zouden betalen.

We besloten vervolgens om dus te blijven staan, uiteraard met de intentie om netjes te betalen. Context; wij zijn een stel van 35 en 34 jaar met een vaste baan uit Nederland, we doen een korte roadtrip langs de east coast en hebben altijd netjes betaald bij elke plek.

We stonden er net 30 min, vooraan zodat we goed zichtbaar waren. Toen kwam plotseling de eigenaar met zijn vrouw op een zeer aggressieve houding op ons af met zak lampen, zeer intimiderend en ze schreeuwde direct dat we direct moesten gaan. Ze waren onredelijk en wilde niet in discussie gaan. Ik zei heel duidelijk dat ik nu wil betalen en anders morgen ochtend, ik liet mijn geld zien. Die vrouw van die man wilde gelijk geld hebben, ik heb ze vervolgens 20 dollar gegeven voor het beetje water wat we hebben gebruikt (want ze hadden blijkbaar heel veel financiële schade opgelopen). Achteraf was dit natuurlijk niet eerlijk en hebben ze mij onder druk gezet. Wat als we kinderen bij ons hadden gehad, of als we een ouder stel waren, wat dan…

Tip, zet een duidelijke slagboom neer, print GROOT uit dat je geen bezoekers wilt na 17.00 en zet een grote waakhond neer en gebruik de volgende keer een taser, dat past bij type mensen zoals jullie het beste.

Wat ik niet snap is dat aardig zijn / vriendelijk blijven niks kost. De manier hoe agressief ze reageren is echt bizar te noemen. Mijn vriendin is zeer geschrokken en heeft moeten huilen naar aanleiding van deze aanvaring. Beschamend! Een cursus vriendelijkheid en behulpzaamheid zou ik jullie adviseren. Pas later drong tot ons door wat ons overkomen was. Ik zou deze vreselijke eigenaren ten allen tijden vermijden. Ze moeten zich kapot schamen!

Persoonlijkheid en karakter is iets wat je hebt en niet kan kopen, hebben deze 2 eigenaren laten zien.. we hebben er nogmaals geen goede woorden voor over, bah!
Very unkind owners. We were confirmed again that it is about personality and character and empathy which is completely lacking. What a misery. I have no words for it.... I would avoid this place.

Great place quiet well off Hway. Very pleasant helpful hosts. Facilities all clean & updated. Nothing old or rundown even an excellent lending libary for swaping books. Fresh filtered tank water for drinking. Could not fault camp ground with large well kept sites. Walk to beach or drive.

Lovely park and location. The beach is walking distance and they are dog friendly.  Lots of rules and not allowed to take a shower from 10-11:30 which seemed like an exorbitant time for the freshly mopped floors to dry.😀

Very clique'
Wouldn't go back
Owners were rude and arrogant and seemed to only care about their customers that have been going back for the last 15 years

Good location though, close to Dingo Beach and Cape Gloucester.

40 minutes out of Airline.

I'll start from the top
10/10 for location
10/10 for facilities pool camp kitchen etc.
8/10 for amenities. Very clean and hot showers
6/10 for powered sites, crammed in like sardines.
6/10 unpowered sites, large but very noisy due to being right on a busy road.
3/10 Customer service. Managers had about as much personality as a stone fish. Just being there seemed a huge deal to them both. Don't think I saw a smile from either of them.
My review was after a 5 night stay in an unpowered site. Awake each morning very early due to road noise. Mainly tradies speeding through the 60zone.
Overall we enjoyed our stay as the park was more about location for our visit.

Well situated and close to the beach. Friendly patrons and friendly owners made us feel welcome. Spotless amenities, camp kitchen and pool area spotless. Each site well maintained and whole park extremely well kept. Nightly happy hour held around a warm fire.
Idyllic; quiet and spacious camping
Definitely a gem of a place , thoroughly enjoyed our stay, very relaxing and peaceful location. Staff were very friendly and helpful, especially the lady on the scooter 🛴

I used to come to this park when I was growing up, 20ish years ago. Unfortunately can’t access the beach with a car from the rear of the park anymore.
Park was full of lovely people. Especially love that the fire is lit everyday by the owners for everyone to enjoy.
The communal showers really need an update to the hot water. I was adjusting the taps literally every 10 seconds in attempt to maintain a comfortable shower temperature for my kids and I.

On my first night, my mate was cooking on the communal fire bbq, the bbq was struggling to cook the thicker fish fillets. We added a small log and stoked the bbq. The lady owner asked what he was cooking and said “ah don’t you think they’re done now”. They were very clearly not. Then said “I saw you put a log in my fire, do you need that much heat?”
My mate gently replied with the obvious answers.
A few minutes later the lady owner began asking another guest “whose empty bottles were on the table?” And “don’t you think they would be better in the bin over there”
The owners walked off after that and came back roughly 30 minutes later to call my mate aside to have a chat.
They had a conversation about how visitors were only permitted until 9pm and that rules were there to be followed. It was only 8:30 at the time. We had a visitor, whom was a local that lived down the road and had simply come down to say hello and have some dinner.

Also thought it was quite ironic that I overheard another guest receive a reminder to slow down in the park, when the lady owner flys around in her mower and electric scooter, much much faster than the signed “5km”

It seems to be a consistent interaction that guests have when a certain person decides to involve themselves.
It’s sad that I had some great memories and experiences dampened by some poor interactions repeatedly by a single person.
No other issues with any of the staff. I think the guest interactions need to be handled by the hired staff, reading previous reviews, it’s clear they’re not handled well by the owners.

Great location, and the people who stay at the Caravan park are often quite lovely and have a good conversation around the fire. I’m a local who lives just in past Hideaway Bay at Captain Jack Drive, and had a friend visiting at the caravan park. I dropped in to say hello to him, and was chatting around the fire to residents and the lady owner came over, pulled him aside and started lecturing him about having me as a guest, when he didn’t bring me in, nor did she approach me and ask who I was. Apparently I was there after curfew, at 8:35pm, and instead of approaching me decided to embarrass and lecture a friend. That’s also after donating some local produce to the caravan park a week earlier.

Cross your i’s and dot your t’s because the lady is like the principal off the movie Matilda.
Amazing park pool awsome location so quiet , staff very friendly a must do

Fantastic bush setting caravan park.This is a well run and managed park. The managers are very professional and friendly enough. This park seems to have a lot of rules but so do all well managed caravan parks - the rules are for the general good of every one in  the park and makes it a pleasant stay. The beach is about a  7 minute walk from the caravan park and the nearest hotel is about a 30 minute nearly 2 k walk by google maps . Both places you need to work out the best way to get to them by the short cuts
The boat ramp is at Dingo Bay about a 7 kilometre drive. There is basically no shops just a small general store in Dingo Bay.The caravan park is a amazing place to stay very clean amenities and a swimming pool. The  caravan sites have plenty of room to set up easily and fully serviced. This caravan park is on septic tanks and tank water so you must be considerate to all others at the park.
Looking forward to coming back. You should book before attending.
Great spot, very relaxed, close to beach, Cape Gloucester and Dingo Beach

Showers and toilets have just had a cheap makeover to justify ridiculous staying price. Water turns off mid shower and there is clearly a lack of ammenities to service the amount of people staying. Your bomb barded with rules from the minute you check in but it’s hard to see and consideration given to the people paying to keep their doors open. A simple example is no hand towel or hand dryer in the bathrooms!! It is an ok location but miles from everything it seems.

We rang and booked over the phone the afternoon we stayed The manager waited to book Us in And was very friendly and efficient
The facilities were immaculate the amenities, the camp kitchen, the pool everything slick and span!
We would stay again!

Poorest communication I’ve ever seen, and that’s coming from a local. I’d probably have better luck booking something like this overseas. Owner Ian is a good bloke so hopefully you speak to him but apologises in advance if you get Cathy, expect total confusion and complete rudeness. Would never bother going there again for the fortune they charge and the troubles they cause.

Neil is not pleasant….but oh my god, Kathy is the devil!! Don’t dare have one single complaint because you will be met with the most indignant and in compassionate response known to the travelling community!
If you’re easy going to the extent that you don’t care about your accommodation…you’ll have NO trouble here!…And also, if you think you may be a person who can accept below par accommodation and just grin and bear it…this may be the place for you!!👍🏼….that said….good luck, you were warned!!
I had a bad experience here, the bathroom are so dirty, the electricity same times don't work well and the owner is disrespectful.
Wonderful people and beautiful area thanks again
Nice quiet caravan park location. Friendly managers

This is one of the best caravan parks we have stayed in. Green grass, conctete slab for our van annex. Pristine amenities block, lovely gardens, and the serene setting encourage s relaxation.
Hosts Neil & Kathy are welcoming and great company around the evening fire pit, all of the regular guests make you feel  part of the group.
Love the pool, location, walks and fishing.
Such a hidden gem.
An absolute credit to Neil & Kathy.
I didn't want to rate this as I'd rather keep secret but what a perfect spot away from the hustle and bustle of Airley beach.

At hideaway bay resort for our first night was fine although a disrespectful, impolite , indesisive female tells us that we cannot go walk around the fire , we left afte she told us woth her body language we cannot roast marshmellows as she said "we keep our gardens nice so you wont find any sticks in there "we,d go have some dinner as the respectful minor i am i then i would put on some youtube for the lovely guests having some dinner and not sitting in silence , the next morning we went out for the day came back to camp at around 6:00pm start to cook dinner 30 mins later and this rude woman comes up and said that I am not allowed to watch some YouTube and we apparently throwing around  a book which is the hideaway bay library to her standards-based on what she said it was mothers day and my mum would go and take my sister to the toilets then when she got back I was cooking on there very sticky bbqs they provide rude woman goes to my mum (on mothers day your child ID not allowed to cook at the BBQ areas my mum said he's very quite capable at doing things rude woman goes" how old is he " mum says 13 ", she like ohh yeaaa 13 so basically at this very wonderful nice place done a great job at doing the setup but oh no
No swimming over 6:30pm
No pulling stupid cardboard off of the TV remove so you see buttons
No music
No child's touching books
No staying up till 9:00
NO being up before 7:00am
And definitely no breathing to loud based on she will come over
This woman is got to be the most rudest hosts of a camp site I've been at I would go to another place to be honest lovely areas  , 5 km away from the beaches ,awesome fishing ,cannot have your own fires , woman does not approve no shoes around tiny fireplace recomend if you wanna get yelled at

Don't stay here with kids. Especially kids that are capable of cooking and fixing the service issues on the TV because the will take the remotes. Don't expect to be able to have a fire in your own area. Your expected to go convene in the commune fire area along as you fit in with "her" own longgggg list of rules and recommendations
...and definitely don't think you have kids that like to roast marshmallows...maybe just bring your own stick...just to avoid the conflict! So to sum it up...Don't breathe too much, don't cook if your "underage", don't shower too much, don't use the TV, don't look for sticks in the gardens and don't use the pool. Definitely do not touch the extensive library of love novels they have on offer to read.
The property is beautifully kept, pity its takes one person to ruin a families holiday experience

Neil and Kathy are fantastic hosts. Top of those we have meet in our 5 week motorhome holiday in Aussie.  They have a simply wonderful holiday park that is perfectly maintained, clean, green n still at great price. They took pity on 2 stranded Kiwis when their motorhome tyre blew. We can't speak highly enough of our hosts and other friendly campers. Don't miss out on this hideaway, it's a positively idyllic experience with a beautiful beach not far away. Pat n Maryka,  Kiwi Campers in Oz 🇳🇿

Cathy and Neil own & run a very good Caravan Park. Amenities are spotless and overall the Park is kept in excellent condition. We enjoyed our stay over a 4 day period - and will look forward to staying with Cathy & Neil again...Jo & Darryl

Beautiful park, amenities are good.
Cathy the owner creates a very tense environment, too many rules and you’r constantly under surveillance.
We won’t be back !

An amazing and beautiful park. Amenities, camp kitchen and pool was super clean and well kept. Kath and Neil were super friendly and helpful. Park is close to beach and has a communal fire pit for all to use. This will not be our last time visiting here. Highly recommend

Our stay was delightful. The location is conveniently close to the beaches, and the amenities were clean and well-maintained. The pool was particularly beautiful and well-kept. The park itself is presented in a lovely manner. The couple who manage the park were also a highlight of our experience. They were diligent and friendly, ensuring the safety and enjoyment of all guests. The rules were clearly communicated and aimed at protecting everyone.

I had the most wonderful experience staying at Hydaway Caravan Park. I would gladly give it a 5-star review! From the moment I arrived, I was impressed by the immaculate amenities. Everything was clean, well-maintained, and in perfect working order. It was clear that the staff takes great pride in ensuring a pleasant and comfortable stay for their guests.

One thing that stood out to me was the abundance of information provided upon entry. The staff ensured that all necessary paperwork was signed and explained everything clearly, making the check-in process smooth and hassle-free. It's always a relief when everything is organized and clearly communicated.

The park itself is set on a peaceful 5-acre property, providing a serene and tranquil atmosphere. I truly enjoyed the quiet surroundings, allowing me to relax and unwind during my stay. The peacefulness of the park made it a perfect escape from the hustle and bustle of everyday life.

Lastly, I must commend the staff at Hydaway Caravan Park. They were absolutely lovely and went above and beyond to make me feel welcome. Their friendly and helpful nature made my stay even more enjoyable. It's always a pleasure to encounter such dedicated and caring staff members.

Overall, I highly recommend Hydaway Caravan Park to anyone looking for a fantastic getaway. The immaculate amenities, clear information, and quiet surroundings, coupled with the wonderful staff, make it an excellent choice for a memorable stay. I can't wait to return!

I had the most incredible experience at Hydeaway Caravan Park and I can't stop raving about their exceptional service. From the moment I arrived, I was greeted with warm smiles and genuine hospitality. The staff went above and beyond to ensure that my stay was comfortable and enjoyable.
The level of attention to detail was truly remarkable. The grounds were immaculately maintained, creating a serene and peaceful atmosphere. The facilities were clean and well-maintained, making my stay even more comfortable. I couldn't have asked for a better camping experience.
But what truly set Hydeaway Caravan Park apart was the exceptional service provided by the staff. They were always available and willing to assist with any request or question I had. Whether it was recommending nearby attractions or providing directions, they were knowledgeable and eager to help.
Overall, my experience at Hydeaway Caravan Park was nothing short of exceptional. The staff's dedication to providing outstanding service, coupled with the beautiful surroundings and well-maintained facilities, created a truly memorable camping experience. I highly recommend Hydeaway Caravan Park to anyone looking for a top-notch camping experience with exceptional service. We are booking our Owners Caravan Group function here for those reasons. Thank you Hydeaway Caravan Park.

Very expensive for an unpowered campsite at $63 p/n. We had young kids under 4 and everything was all good until we checked out and had the female owner Kathy call and ask to speak to myself, She was incredibly rude and disrespectful towards me over the phone. I was packing up our camp on the last night well before the noise curfew so we could check out early the next morning, during the phone call she was cranky because of the excessive noise that was apparently coming from my family’s campsite. She was also incredibly disrespectful towards me in regards to me trying to keep my kids quiet after the noise curfew for the other guests but my children were in bed before 9pm and she said she had complaints made about unruly behaviour from myself by other guests and on the phone call she said she made some very inappropriate and unprofessional remarks about myself and my children which was all recorded on the phone call. And as for these complaints they are completely false because the car we were travelling in has hard wired dash cams front and back that constantly record video and audio and when confronted about these allegations the owner Kathy hasn’t responded to my email. She has also discriminated against myself by saying I’m not allowed to book in to stay there with my family again but that is fine after my experience with her I will never be back and will never recommend her park to any of my friends or anyone who asks for a place to stay around dingo beach. Definitely not a family friendly place to stay and we got the feeling she doesn’t like family’s or children staying at her park. Also at check in the lady behind the counter spent more time explaining the can’t do around the park and basically that kids can’t be kids. Would give a zero star if I possibly could. I don’t know how anyone could have possibly complained about children being children because the closet person staying near us was around 200m away from our campsite
Clean and accessible park
We'll kept caravan park

This place is amazing. The grounds are exceptional, the facilities are always clean and tidy. The owners Neil and Kathy are awesome. 10 out of 10.
Thanks guys for a great holiday.

Best Camping in the Whit Sundays, well maintained facilities. Nice shower and toilet block, really nice camp kitchen. large sites, powerd and unpowered. swimming pool and small convenience store. Really close to the beach.

Great spot walking distance from the beach and pub, good for a camp on a windy day in a sheltered area. Friendly staff (Cathy) and clean hot&cold showers and toilets, would recommend to anyone who does not want to spend an arm and a leg to camp on the waterfront.
Very beautiful area and great facilities, but the staff were cranky, unmanageable and didn’t even seem like they wanted us there.
Pleasure staying in this park was everything l hoped for and more. Great location central to everything l wanted to do . Would definitely come back and recommend to friends and family

Nice camping grounds with both powered and unpowered sites. Not suitable for tents IMO. All sites are angled a bit wich works for a campers but not for a tent.
Very clean amenities, pool on site.
Over 500 meters walk to the beach.
Staff is helpful and welcoming.
Look into the cancelation policy before booking as there is no refunds or credit given unless you cancel at least 2 weeks prior your booking.

Nice facilities, but the staff are incredibly rude, there are signs everywhere telling you what not to do, very unwelcoming atmosphere. Lucky for us this was just an overnight stay. Would not recommend to anyone.

Really expensive camping area, 43$ for unpowered site.
Toilets were okay, not really clean and a bit old, same for the showers.
5min showers only, you need to mop after yourself.

Everything is forbidden and has rules.
Camp kitchen is clean, but no noise or whatsoever tolerated.
Ridiculous we drove out of our way kilometres only to find park closed. No warning. Not happy
Lovely and clean

Lovely caravan park, clean and good location. Little bit weird with their pool - asked to check out at 11am, they said no issue, went to put feet in pool whilst family was packing down Van, got rudely yelled out. Relax guys - you have a beautiful park. Relax a little more. All-in-all, nice park!

We had stopped here 6 years ago and enjoyed it so decided to try it again and it didn't disappoint.  It was even better this time as the kitchen was way better and there is now a very refreshing, shaded pool. Amenities were the same and worked fine.  Had a nice night at the fire pit with some fellow campers. Really enjoyed the walks to the local beaches.

Big shout out to Cathy and Neil owners of caravan park , made us feel very welcome , this would have to be one of the best places to visit on East coast of Australia. The position of this park is awesome so much to see and do , we highly recommend this park, very sad to be leaving but will definitely be returning next year , Thanx so much Guys

Such a beautiful location and very tidy caravan park. However I would not recommend for families with young children, the rules that are barked at you are unreasonable and downright ridiculous! The lady owner does not seem to like children at all. No spashling, talking or doing anything fun by the pool! My well behaved children got told off while showering after a swim...they were literally just standing there rinsing off! Signs everywhere about all the rules you must adhere too.
We still had a lovely time exploring the area but would only recommend this caravan park if you don't have children.
Quite little Camping Area. Beautiful white sandy beaches close by.

This park is in a good spot with nice big sites that are well maintained, though definitely has got a ‘don’t touch my stuff’ vibe, with signs everywhere telling you what not to do which is super unwelcoming.
Also expensive!
The bathrooms were not that clean.
It’s not kid friendly at all, think it would be better suited to an over 55’s park.
Lovely, lush campsite. Very well looked after. Clean, modern facilities and friendly staff.

Well maintained park just a short drive to a couple of beautiful hidden gem beaches. Amenities were clean and the camp kitchen and pool new. Great community campfire too. Great place to stay for a night,  two or even a week.

One of the best parks we have stayed in. Very friendly owners who work extremely hard to maintain well kept grounds. Amazing location. Great price. Very clean bathrooms. Kid and pet friendly. Beautiful beaches and nice pubs and resorts close by. This was the highlight of our 10 week holiday on the East Coast of Australia. We can't wait to come back. Such a chilled out vibe and I'm leaving feeling relaxed and rejuvenated. Highly reccomend.
Beautiful  place to stay with friendly welcoming and great amenities. Short walk to beach.

My husband and I travelled from Brisbane for our honeymoon, this was one of our stops along the way. We stayed here for 3 nights at an unpowered site and wish we could have stayed longer!
The owners are so friendly and make you feel at home, the amenities are always clean and very well maintained. There is a beautiful pool and amazing camp kitchen/communal area. The park was booked out and we had no issues with noise or being too crowed. Just a short walk to the beach which is incredible view!
We will 100% be back in the near future we hope. Would recommend to anyone to stop here.

Friendly and highly accommodating owners/staff. Far away from anything busy, this supreme park offers family travellers the opportunity to step back into a quieter and easy paced life.
Excellent amenities, clean camp kitchen, clean bathrooms and very tidy grounds. Lovely shaded pool, which os heated on winter mornings, meaning late swims are possible.

Can’t recommend staying over at Neil and Cathy’s park enough.
Nice quite park. Grassed sites. And Cape Gloucester has an amazing beach 3 min away. And still 45 min to Airlie

What a beautiful spot to stay. Loving our time here. Big sites and so quiet. Friendly, like minded neighbours around us. Facilities are spotless, the camp kitchen is amazing, chef standards! We loved our swim in the pool today. Kathy welcomed us yesterday and gave us all the local info we needed. Can't wait to come back next year, the perfect location,  a 10 minute walk to the beach. So nice to be able to swim at the beach without worrying about crocs...and not have to worry about getting eaten alive by midgies. What a hidden gem!
Friendly and well laid out

This caravan park  is lovely and the facilities are clean and well maintained , however it is not a family friendly park and kids are definitely not welcome. The pool area is for people who make a very conscious  effort to not make a splash “waste water” and you are watched at all times. The signs everywhere make you feel like you are walking on eggshells and being watched and policed at all times. We won’t be returning unfortunately.
